meaning: достичь высокого положения несмотря на системные барьеры
example: This spring forum honored women who broke the glass ceiling in tech.
hint: разбить невидимую преграду и впустить свет

meaning: приложить дополнительные усилия ради отличного результата
example: He went the extra mile helping with the March 8 volunteer event.

meaning: вдохновлять действиями, а не только словами
example: Our coach leads by example during spring community projects.
hint: не говорить о действиях, а показывать

example: She stood her ground in the March hiring policy discussion.
At the spring meetup, everyone was encouraged to speak their mind.
The team rose to the occasion before the March 8 conference.

meaning: начать активно и эффективно с первых минут
example: After the spring break, interns hit the ground running.
Small spring initiatives moved the needle on inclusion.
Each volunteer brought something valuable to the table this March.
Many men stepped up to support spring mentorship sessions.

meaning: встать на чью-то сторону и помочь
example: Colleagues backed her up during the spring pitch.
In good teams, people always have each other's back.
She held her own in a high-pressure spring debate.
The spring campaign succeeded because they thought outside the box.
Her March keynote set the bar high for young leaders.
Scholarships opened doors for many students this spring.
One mentoring hour can make a difference in someone's career.
He took the lead in organizing a spring education fair.
Her clear plan won the entire panel over.
March discussions helped build bridges across departments.
By April, the whole team was on the same page.
Women engineers were in the spotlight during spring events.
In this project, local mentors call the shots.

meaning: не терять прогресс и продолжать движение
example: After March 8, teams kept the momentum going all spring.
Open feedback turned the tide in company culture this spring.

meaning: создать что-то полностью с самого начала
example: They built a mentorship app from the ground up in March.

meaning: помогать другим в ответ на полученную помощь
example: Graduates paid it forward by mentoring spring interns.
The spring startup challenge pushed the envelope in education.
Leaders walked the talk by funding new scholarships this spring.
A kind message can lift someone up before a March presentation.
